title: Discrete math questions for Jackson — graph-theory study (2022)
html:
Discrete math questions for Jackson — graph-theory study (2022)

Source: 2022-06-06 Apple Note. Office-hours prep questions for "Jackson" (TA or peer) — discrete math / graph theory topics. CS-pivot evidence.

TL;DR

  • Adjacency matrix questions: weighted vs. multi-edge representation; how to encode multiple edges in a matrix.
  • Listed topics for clarification: binomial theorem extent, handshake theorem, bipartite graphs, "Hall's theorem?", Ore's vs. Dirac's theorem, incidence matrix (edges × vertices).
  • Mid-2022 timestamp: Yahir mid-CS-pivot (OSU CS major track), grinding the discrete-math foundations that come before James Cassidy's data-structures era and CS162.
  • "Jackson" = the Jackson who appears as TA/peer math collaborator; same "Jackson Frazier wetlands" mentioned in Ahmad Jamal stream is a place not the person.
  • Evidence-of-grind: a study-questions list as the artifact, not the answers — Yahir captures the frontier of his confusion as a working tool.

Connections

  • osu
  • asking the right question — operational instance
  • 2022 12 16 james cassidy be afraid to be silent — adjacent CS-prof figure
  • systems thinking
